intro: Chamaerhodiola scabrida, also known as the desert rhodiola, is a perennial plant species in the family Crassulaceae, native to the mountainous regions of China and Mongolia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to alleviate stress, improve mood, and enhance physical performance due to its adaptogenic properties. The bioactive constituents of Chamaerhodiola scabrida include rhodiola glycosides, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Chamaerhodiola scabrida include extracts, capsules, and teas, often used to promote mental clarity, reduce fatigue, and enhance overall well-being.
